title: Establishing a quantitative link between crystal violet absorbance and biomass in biofilms
author: Stephens, Mahadevan, et al.

steps.title: Cultivate bacterial cells in suspended culture according to strain-specific protocols
steps.instruction: Cultivate bacterial cells in suspended culture according to strain-specific protocols.

steps.title: Prepare a dilution series (minimum 23...
steps.instruction: Prepare a dilution series (minimum 23 mL per sample) covering a range of OD values (e.g., 0.1 to 2.0).

steps.title: Quantify planktonic cells
steps.instruction:
Quantify planktonic cells:
a.Measure 1 mL of each dilution in a spectrophotometer to confirm OD.b.Measure 100 µL of each dilution in a plate reader (e.g., OD600 for Escherichia coli).

steps.title: Determine dry cell weight (DCW)
steps.instruction:
Determine dry cell weight (DCW):
a.Pre-weigh filter paper or membrane.b.Filter 20 mL of each sample through the membrane and dry to constant weight (air-dried or in oven).c.Re-weigh and subtract initial mass to determine DCW.

steps.title: Measure crystal violet absorbance
steps.instruction:
Measure crystal violet absorbance:
a.Transfer 1 mL of each dilution to an Eppendorf tube.b.Centrifuge at 10,000 G to pellet cells.c.Decant supernatant and wash cells with deionized water.d.Repeat centrifugation and decant.e.Add 250 µL of 0.1 % crystal violet and resuspend.f.Centrifuge and decant excess stain.g.Gently wash the pellet by submerging the pellet in a beaker of clean deionized water (being careful not to spill into clean water) and decant into waste container repeating until rinse water runs clean.h.Add 375 µL of solvent (ethanol or acetic acid) to each tube and resuspend.i.Centrifuge and transfer 100 µL of supernatant to a microtiter plate.j.Repeat for all samples.k.Include wells for blanks.l.Measure absorbance at 540 nm using a plate reader.

steps.title: Analyze data by plotting (ensure plate...
steps.instruction:
Analyze data by plotting (ensure plate reader data is normalized to blanks):
a.Plate reader OD vs. spectrophotometer ODb.Spectrophotometer OD vs. DCWc.Crystal violet OD vs. DCWd.Crystal violet OD vs. spectrophotometer OD
